w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: the next serious module is the
Not optional. It is one of the core workflow components of your whole project.
In the build order, it comes immediately after leave approvals:
THEN → Supervisor assigns and monitors tasks
Your tasks table already has the structure:
So the real next move is NOT randomly coding pages.
You need to implement this in order:
If you skip structure and jump to UI first, you will create garbage architecture and rewrite everything later.
Your clean implementation order should be:
The dangerous mistake here is treating tasks like a simple todo list.
And enforce transitions instead of letting employees freely set anything.
That kind of discipline is what separates a real enterprise system from a student CRUD project.
“what about hr can not assign task and from there i needed to add something thats daily log task for employee and supervisor where supervisor inspect employee tasks and supervisor inspected by hr”
Good. Now you are moving from a basic attendance app into an actual organizational workflow system.
But your current design still has a structural weakness:
HR only manages people and reports but has zero operational oversight.
That becomes unrealistic in real institutions.
You are right to question it.
If HR can assign tasks directly to employees, you destroy the chain of command.
So HR should NOT behave like another supervisor.
2. Your Daily Log Idea is VERY Strong
This is actually one of the best ideas you have mentioned so far.
That is far stronger academically and architecturally.
That gives your project depth most student systems never reach.
This should be separate from tasks.
Those are NOT the same thing.
A daily log is what happened TODAY.
Now your system has layered accountability.
This is cleaner than stuffing everything into one table.
submits daily log before end of day
HR should inspect SUPERVISION QUALITY — not micromanage employee tasks.
Do NOT turn daily logs into essays.
Students always ruin this module by making:
→ employee daily log can reference task progress.
→ supervisor sees lateness + low productivity correlation.
10. Your Project Is Becoming Much Stronger
That is a serious final year project now.
“and another thing i need daily log module to be produced as pdf at the end of the week and can be downloaded for paper based workflow”
Now your system is no longer just digital workflow management.
You are solving a REAL Tanzanian institutional problem:
Digital processing + printable paper-based compliance workflow.
That is exactly how many government and semi-government institutions actually operate:
This makes your project much more realistic.
But you need to design this carefully or the PDF feature becomes useless decoration.
A real institutional weekly report must look like an official document.
Your weekly log PDF should look like:
Do NOT generate PDFs directly from raw daily logs every time.
This is how real systems behave.
Then your system needs TWO levels:
Otherwise your PDFs will render horribly.
9. Your System Is Now Becoming a Full Institutional Management Platform
This is MUCH stronger than your original proposal.
Do NOT keep randomly adding features forever.
You are close to feature explosion.
Your system now has enough depth for:
Because bloated systems collapse under poor structure.
